title: PEER SUPPORT SERVICES - 211info
text: 2ND CHANCE SHELTER PEER SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services such as limited assistance obtaining Oregon ID, mailing addresses, GED tutoring, and essential supplies as available (such as toothpaste, shampoo, soap, and deodorant). Also provides job search assistance such as preparing a resume, completing job applications, and interview techniques. Other services include: counseling including anger management, detox and rehab referrals, Oregon Health Plan enrollment assistance, and computer and internet access. 1100 Jackson Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 967-8545 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 967-8545 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Benton, Lincoln, and Linn Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Services: Anger Management Certificates/Forms Assistance GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction Individual Counseling Medicaid Information/Counseling Personal/Grooming Supplies Prejob Guidance Public Internet Access Sites Substance Use Disorder Referrals Temporary Mailing Address Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - EMERGENCY SHELTER If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. 2ND CHANCE SHELTER
